title: Can't find column for join query
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->
```sql
CREATE TABLE `DD` (

  `col_bigint_not_null_key` bigint NOT NULL,
  `col_decimal_30_10` decimal(30,10) DEFAULT NULL,

CREATE TABLE `L` (
  `col_decimal_key` decimal(10,0) DEFAULT NULL,
  `col_double_key` double DEFAULT NULL,
  `col_varchar_binary` varbinary(1) DEFAULT NULL,
  `col_int` int DEFAULT NULL,

  `col_varchar_64_binary_not_null_key` varbinary(64) NOT NULL,

) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin;
SELECT     * FROM     DD AS table1     join (         G AS table2         join L AS table3 ON table3.col_decimal_30_10 = table2.col_int_key     ) ON table3.col_varchar_binary = table2.col_varchar_binary_key;
```
### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
success
### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
```sql
mysql> SELECT     * FROM     DD AS table1     join (         G AS table2         join L AS table3 ON table3.col_decimal_30_10 = table2.col_int_key     ) ON table3.col_varchar_binary = table2.col_varchar_binary_key;
ERROR 1105 (HY000): Can't find column test.g.col_bigint_not_null_key in schema Column: [test.g.col_varchar_binary_key,test.g.pk] PKOrUK: [] NullableUK: []
```

INSERT INTO B (ref_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Description 1'), (2, NULL);

key_bug_features: Join query fails due to missing column in schema
